plan_analysis:
The meta-question hinges on whether the Metaculus community probability for “Starmer out in 2026” rises (or at least stays) above 63 percent during the eight-day window ending 11 Mar 2026.  
Information already supplied: the current CP (65 %), its recent trend (+2 pp in 30 days), and general political context (polling slump, rumoured challengers).  
Key gaps for forecasters:

1. Base-rate / volatility data: How fast and how far do Metaculus community probabilities typically move in the final week before a question’s checkpoint or close? Also, historic frequency of UK PMs leaving office mid-term or being forced out by their own party.
2. Resolution mechanics: Confirmation of the precise data time-stamp the site will use and how often the CP auto-updates (hourly? daily?).
3. Near-term drivers (next 8 days): a) Scheduled political events likely to shift perceptions (Spring Budget 2026 date, by-elections, major polling releases). b) Emerging news or leaks on Labour leadership manoeuvres or Starmer’s health.
4. Contrarian evidence: Signs that Starmer is safer than assumed—e.g.\ a strong poll, supportive Budget reception, or quashed challenge rumours.

The search strategy therefore combines:
• Historical queries on Metaculus prediction drift and UK leadership ousters (5 queries ≈ 60 %).  
• Current-news queries on imminent events, polls, and rumours (3 queries ≈ 40 %).  
• One broader [HISTORICAL] Agent query to surface scholarly or analytical syntheses of past PM removals and their lead-indicators.

queries_planned.intent: Find studies or blog posts quantifying typical week-to-week percentage-point moves in Metaculus community forecasts, to establish a base rate of >2 pp swings.

queries_planned.query: UK prime ministers resigned while in office stats
queries_planned.intent: Retrieve historical data on how often and why UK PMs have left office mid-parliament to benchmark the substantive probability.

queries_planned.query: labour leadership challenge procedure 20 percent mps threshold
queries_planned.intent: Clarify formal mechanics/timing of ousting a Labour PM, indicating how quickly such moves could unfold and be priced in.
Locate examples/analyses of last-minute sentiment shifts on Metaculus questions to gauge variance risk.

queries_planned.query: factors preceding uk pm removal case studies
queries_planned.intent: Surface comparative analyses of past UK PM departures (e.g., Thatcher 1990, Blair 2007, Johnson 2022) and identify lead indicators (poll slumps, local-election losses, budget fallout) relevant to Starmer. (Agent)
Confirm whether the Budget will occur within the 3-11 March window, a potential catalyst for new information and forecast movement.
Capture any fresh reporting or leaks about coup attempts, letters, or MPs rallying behind challengers in the last few days.

queries_planned.query: UK voting intention poll March 2026 Labour Reform
queries_planned.intent: Collect the newest public-opinion polls or internal leaks released this week that could sway perceptions of Starmer’s vulnerability.
